answer:
The key terms and conditions governing payment obligations vary by agreement:

Under the Service Agreement:
*   Client must pay Service Provider net 30 days from the invoice date (service_agreement.html, p.1).
*   Late payments will incur a 1.5% monthly interest (service_agreement.html, p.1).
*   All payments must be in USD (service_agreement.html, p.1).
*   All work product created by the Service Provider is assigned to the Client upon full payment (service_agreement.html, p.1).
*   Maximum liability is capped at fees paid in the preceding 12 months (service_agreement.html, p.1).

Under the Commercial Lease Agreement:
*   Tenant must pay $15,000 per month on the first day of each month (lease_contract.html, p.1).
*   A grace period of 5 days applies, after which a late fee of $500 per day will be charged (lease_contract.html, p.1).
*   If the premises are rendered unusable by natural disaster, the rent obligation is suspended (lease_contract.html, p.1).
*   Tenant may terminate with 90 days notice and a penalty of 3 months' rent (lease_contract.html, p.1).

Under the Non-Disclosure Agreement:
*   Breach of the NDA entitles the Disclosing Party to seek liquidated damages of $500,000 (nda_agreement.html, p.1).
